mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that historically precede earnings downgrades, depressing equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE exceeds 4.0% for two consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a decline in consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 5.0 percent, aggressive federal funds rate hikes fail to anchor expectations, causing real rates to spike and equity multiples to contract sharply over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0% and FEDFUNDS rises concurrently over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.04 across all rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.3 million units, it signals a sharp cooling in the construction sector and broader consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST drops below 1.3 million units for two consecutive months (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and declining payroll employment indicates a labor market inflection point that historically triggers recessionary earnings revisions, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration of UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of historical instances over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent, elevated consumer credit outstanding amplifies spending momentum, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive premium when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% and RSAFS rises concurrently over a 3-year rolling window.
